channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18315] LNet: route is not created if socklnd NID is added dynamically using "lnetctl --nid"
item.description:
ksocklnd-config doesn't get executed if NID is added dynamically as follows

lnetctl net add --nid 192.168.122.50@tcp

If the NID is added using the following command

lnetctl net add --net tcp --if ens3

then ksocklnd-config does get executed and adds the route which ensures that traffic sent using the new NID is going to be sent using the intended interface.

The behaviour is the same for IPv4 and IPv6 NIDs.
item.summary: LNet: route is not created if socklnd NID is added dynamically using "lnetctl --nid"

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
This issue has been resolved by Cyril in:
LU-18135 scripts: call ksocklnd-config with IPv6
https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/61649

The previous IPv6 test results were based on the ex/lustre-release codebase.
The issue is resolved in fs/lustre-release, with both IPv4 and IPv6 tests passing.

Hi cbordage , please close this ticket. Thanks!

"Xinxia Qu <xqu@ddn.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/63194
Subject: LU-18315 lnet: Route is not created for IPv6 NID
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 406f269ffbed2f3ed99de63843c061588d484724

Test Observations

1. IPv4 NIDs work fine. Both commonds trigger ksocklnd-config, and add policy routing.
2. IPv6 NIDs have issues. Both commonds do not trigger ksocklnd-config.

Commands for IPv4

strace -f lnetctl net add --nid 192.168.122.50@tcp 2>&1 | grep ksocklnd-config
strace -f lnetctl net add --net tcp --if ens38 2>&1 | grep ksocklnd-config

Commands for IPv6

strace -f lnetctl net add --nid 2001:db8::1@tcp 2>&1 | grep ksocklnd-config
strace -f lnetctl net add --net tcp --if ens38 2>&1 | grep ksocklnd-config

Version

[root@vmlr810i lustre-release]# git log --oneline -1
20de492fda (HEAD -> master, tag: v2_16_0-RC5, tag: 2.16.0-RC5, origin/master, origin/HEAD) New RC 2.16.0-RC5

[root@vmlr810i lustre-release]# git branch
* master 

Analysis

The IOC_LIBCFS_GET_LOCAL_NI operation does not return IPv6 NIDs when fetching the NI.

Test Conclusion

1. It appears that both of these addition methods trigger the execution of `ksocklnd-config`.
2. During its execution, `ksocklnd-config` adds ip route & rule for the net.

Using `strace` to Verify `ksocklnd-config` Execution

Test Commands

strace -f lnetctl net add --nid 192.168.122.50@tcp 2>&1 | grep ksocklnd-config
strace -f lnetctl net add --net tcp --if ens38 2>&1 | grep ksocklnd-config

System Call Trace for `lnetctl net add --nid 192.168.122.50@tcp`

[root@vmlr810i ~]# strace -f lnetctl net add --nid 192.168.122.50@tcp 2>&1 | grep ksocklnd-config
[pid  1949] execve("/bin/sh", ["sh", "-c", "/usr/sbin/ksocklnd-config ens38"], 0x7fff20343468 /* 41 vars */) = 0
[pid  1949] execve("/usr/sbin/ksocklnd-config", ["/usr/sbin/ksocklnd-config", "ens38"], 0x55f41cc11e90 /* 41 vars */) = 0
[pid  1949] openat(AT_FDCWD, "/usr/sbin/ksocklnd-config", O_RDONLY) = 4
[pid  1949] stat("/usr/sbin/ksocklnd-config", {st_mode=S_IFREG|0755, st_size=13023, ...}) = 0
[pid  1966] execve("/usr/bin/logger", ["logger", "ksocklnd-config:", "/sbin/ip", "route", "flush", "table", "ens38"], 0x5618f51468c0 /* 41 vars */) = 0
[pid  1966] sendmsg(2, {msg_name=NULL, msg_namelen=0, msg_iov=[{iov_base="<13>Dec 24 22:55:11 root: ", iov_len=26}, {iov_base="ksocklnd-config: /sbin/ip route "..., iov_len=49}], msg_iovlen=2, msg_controllen=0, msg_flags=0}, MSG_NOSIGNAL) = 75
[pid  2103] execve("/usr/bin/logger", ["logger", "ksocklnd-config:", "/sbin/ip", "route", "add", "192.168.122.0/24", "dev", "ens38", "proto", "kernel", "scope", "link", "src", "192.168.122.50", "table", "ens38"], 0x5618f51468c0 /* 41 vars */) = 0
[pid  2103] sendmsg(4, {msg_name=NULL, msg_namelen=0, msg_iov=[{iov_base="<13>Dec 24 22:55:13 root: ", iov_len=26}, {iov_base="ksocklnd-config: /sbin/ip route "..., iov_len=117}], msg_iovlen=2, msg_controllen=0, msg_flags=0}, MSG_NOSIGNAL) = 143
[pid  2104] execve("/usr/bin/logger", ["logger", "ksocklnd-config:", "/sbin/ip", "rule", "del", "from", "192.168.122.50", "table", "ens38"], 0x5618f51468c0 /* 41 vars */) = 0
[pid  2104] sendmsg(4, {msg_name=NULL, msg_namelen=0, msg_iov=[{iov_base="<13>Dec 24 22:55:13 root: ", iov_len=26}, {iov_base="ksocklnd-config: /sbin/ip rule d"..., iov_len=66}], msg_iovlen=2, msg_controllen=0, msg_flags=0}, MSG_NOSIGNAL) = 92
[pid  2105] execve("/usr/bin/logger", ["logger", "ksocklnd-config:", "/sbin/ip", "rule", "add", "from", "192.168.122.50", "table", "ens38"], 0x5618f51468c0 /* 41 vars */) = 0
[pid  2105] sendmsg(4, {msg_name=NULL, msg_namelen=0, msg_iov=[{iov_base="<13>Dec 24 22:55:13 root: ", iov_len=26}, {iov_base="ksocklnd-config: /sbin/ip rule a"..., iov_len=66}], msg_iovlen=2, msg_controllen=0, msg_flags=0}, MSG_NOSIGNAL) = 92

We found that the command `lnetctl net add --nid 192.168.122.50@tcp` triggers the execution of `/usr/sbin/ksocklnd-config`.
By observing the subcommands executed in the system calls, we can see that it configures policy routing as follows:

/sbin/ip route flush table ens38
/sbin/ip route add 192.168.122.0/24 dev ens38 proto kernel scope link src 192.168.122.50 table ens38
/sbin/ip rule del from 192.168.122.50 table ens38
/sbin/ip rule add from 192.168.122.50 table ens38

These configurations can be verified during testing by monitoring `ip rule` and `ip route show table <id>`.

System Call Trace for `lnetctl net add --net tcp --if ens38`

The observed behavior is consistent with the above. (Output omitted for brevity.)

Complete Test

We can complete the entire test by following the commands below:

# Initialize
ip addr add 192.168.122.50/24 dev ens38
ip addr show dev ens38ip rule
ip route | grep 192.168.122.0
modprobe lnet
lnetctl lnet configure
lnetctl net show

# Trace
strace -f lnetctl net add --nid 192.168.122.50@tcp 2>&1 | grep ksocklnd-config
lnetctl net show
ip rule
ip route show table ens38

# Cleanup
lnetctl net del --nid 192.168.122.50@tcp
ip route flush table ens38
ip rule del from 192.168.122.50 table ens38
ip rule
ip route show table ens38

# Trace
strace -f lnetctl net add --net tcp --if ens38 2>&1 | grep ksocklnd-config
lnetctl net show
ip rule
ip route show table ens38
